title: Turn Task Graph
description: Multi-task turn interpretation output shape. The interpreter emits an ordered graph of subtasks; the framework walker dispatches them in topological order. A single-intent turn is the degenerate case: one node, no edges.

tasks.description: Task nodes, 1-indexed. Nodes with empty blocked_by are immediately runnable. Nodes whose blocked_by list names incomplete tasks are deferred to pending_tasks in orchestrator state.

task_id.description: Unique task identifier within this turn's graph. 1-indexed, unique, contiguous.

intent.description: Domain-specific intent label for this subtask (e.g. 'weather', 'planning', 'general').

status.description: Execution status of this task node. 'pending' is the initial state from the interpreter. 'blocked' means blocked_by is non-empty and those tasks are not yet completed. The framework sets 'in_progress' when dispatching, 'completed' when the subtask finishes, 'abandoned' on subtask failure (which cascades to dependents).

blocked_by.description: task_id values this node waits for. Empty list = immediately runnable. Referenced task_ids must all exist in the same tasks array. Cycles are not permitted.

turn_data.description: Single-task turn interpretation payload for this subtask. Must conform to the domain's turn_input_schema. Identical field semantics as the flat single-task turn_data dict.

intent_type.description: Primary intent classification for this subtask.

task_status.description: Task lifecycle status for this subtask.

tool_call_requested.description: Whether fulfilling this subtask requires a tool call.

off_task_ratio.description: Fraction of subtask content that is off-topic. Typically 0.0 for individual subtasks (the ratio is distributed across the full message).

examples._comment: Single-intent turn — degenerate case (one node, no edges).
Two-intent turn — weather unlocks trip planning.
